title: acalypha pruinosa
intro: Acalypha pruinosa, also known as the woolly acalypha, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its small white flowers and woolly leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Acalypha pruinosa include treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as providing relief from pain and inflammation. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
